core_mechanism: A neighborhood-limited spreading (contagion) process on a network, in which a quantity propagates from a source through nodes' local neighborhoods and is characterized by the maximal connected fraction of a node's neighbors reachable (a local clustering/neighborhood-overlap measure) together with a propagation time, both governed by node degree and network topology
brief_reason: The abstract defines a 'spread factor' as the average maximal fraction of a node's neighbors that interchange information among each other and a 'spreading time' to reach that fraction, then studies how these spreading quantities depend on connectivity across scale-free and small-world topologies.
